title: Device for driving a plurality of motors and electric apparatus including the same
abstract: A device for driving a plurality of motors, including an inverter connected to a DC terminal; a multi-phase motor connected to the inverter; and a single-phase motor serially connected to the multi-phase motor, wherein a number of frequency of current input to the multi-phase motor when driving the single-phase motor and the multi-phase motor at the same speed is smaller than the number of frequency of current input to the multi-phase motor when driving the single-phase motor and the multi-phase motor at different speeds. Accordingly, a plurality of motors can be simultaneously driven at different speeds, by using a single inverter.
claims: Claims ( 20 ) A device for driving a plurality of motors, the device comprising: an inverter connected to a direct current (DC) terminal; a multi-phase motor connected to the inverter; and a single-phase motor connected to the multi-phase motor, wherein the device is configured to drive the single-phase motor and the multi-phase motor at a same speed by providing a first number of frequencies for current input to the multi-phase motor, wherein the device is configured to drive the single-phase motor and the multi-phase motor at different speeds by providing a second number of frequencies for current input to the multi-phase motor, and wherein the first number is smaller than the second number. The device of claim 1, wherein the device is configured to drive the single-phase motor and the multi-phase motor at a same speed by inputting a current that has a single frequency to the multi-phase motor, and wherein the device is configured to drive the single-phase motor and the multi-phase motor at different speeds by inputting a current that has two or more frequencies to the multi-phase motor. The device of claim 1, wherein the device is configured to drive the single-phase motor and the multi-phase motor at a same speed by corresponding an output current waveform from the inverter to a single sine wave, and wherein the device is configured to drive the single-phase motor and the multi-phase motor at different speeds by corresponding an output current waveform from the inverter to a sum of a plurality of sine waves. The device of claim 1, wherein the device is configured to drive the single-phase motor and the multi-phase motor at different speeds by rotating the multi-phase motor at a first speed that corresponds to a first frequency of a first current input to the multi-phase motor, and wherein the device is configured to rotate the single-phase motor at a second speed that corresponds to a second frequency of a second current input to the single-phase motor. The device of claim 1, wherein the device is configured to drive the single-phase motor and the multi-phase motor at a same speed by driving the multi-phase motor and the single-phase motor by an effective current on a single frequency. The device of claim 1, wherein the device is configured to drive the single-phase motor and the multi-phase motor at a same speed, based on a maximum level of effective current of the multi-phase motor and a maximum level of effective current of the single-phase motor being the same, a torque generated by the multi-phase motor being greater than a torque generated by the single-phase motor. The device of claim 1, wherein the device is configured to drive the single-phase motor and the multi-phase motor at different speeds by driving the multi-phase motor and the single-phase motor by an effective current on different frequencies. The device of claim 1, further comprising a first capacitor and a second capacitor connected between both ends of the DC terminal, wherein a neutral point of the multi-phase motor is connected to one end of the single-phase motor, and another end of the single-phase motor is connected to a node between the first capacitor and the second capacitor. The device of claim 8, further comprising: a first voltage detector configured to detect a DC voltage of both ends of the first capacitor; a second voltage detector configured to detect a DC voltage of both ends of the second capacitor; a first current detector configured to detect a first current input to the multi-phase motor; and a second current detector configured to detect a second current input to the single-phase motor. The device of claim 1, further comprising a controller configured to control the inverter, wherein the controller is configured to control, based on driving the single-phase motor and the multi-phase motor at a same speed, the current input to the multi-phase motor to have a single frequency, and wherein the controller is configured to control, based on driving the single-phase motor and the multi-phase motor at different speeds, the current input to the multi-phase motor to have two or more frequencies. The device of claim 10, wherein the controller is configured to calculate a neutral point voltage of the multi-phase motor. The device of claim 1, wherein the device is configured to drive the single-phase motor and the multi-phase motor simultaneously at different speeds by flowing different currents through the multi-phase motor and the single-phase motor, and wherein, based on only the multi-phase motor being driven, a current flows through the multi-phase motor and no current flows through the single-phase motor. The device of claim 12, wherein the device is configured to drive the single-phase motor and the multi-phase motor simultaneously at different speeds by inputting a first current that has a first frequency and a second frequency to the multi-phase motor, and by inputting a second current that has the second frequency to the single-phase motor. The device of claim 13, wherein the device is configured to drive the single-phase motor and the multi-phase motor simultaneously at the same speed by inputting a third current that has a third frequency to the multi-phase motor and a fourth current that has the third frequency to the single-phase motor. The device of claim 1, further comprising a first capacitor and a second capacitor connected between both ends of the DC terminal, wherein, based on driving at least the single-phase motor from the single-phase motor and the multi-phase motor, a neutral point voltage of the multi-phase motor and a DC terminal neutral point voltage between the first capacitor and the second capacitor are different, and wherein, based on driving only the multi-phase motor, the neutral point voltage of the multi-phase motor and the DC terminal neutral point voltage between the first capacitor and the second capacitor are same. A device for driving a plurality of motors, the device comprising: an inverter connected to a direct current (DC) terminal; a multi-phase motor connected to the inverter; and a single-phase motor connected to the multi-phase motor, wherein the device is configured to drive only the single-phase motor from the single-phase motor and the multi-phase motor by turning on all upper switching elements of the inverter or all lower switching elements of the inverter are turned on. The device of claim 16, wherein, based on all the upper switching elements of the inverter or all the lower switching elements of the inverter being turned on, a level of a first current that has a single frequency and that flows in the multi-phase motor is greater than a level of a second current that has multiple phases and that flows in the multi-phase motor. The device of claim 16, wherein the device is configured to drive only the single-phase motor from the single-phase motor and the multi-phase motor by inputting: a first current to the multi-phase motor and an effective current that flows through the multi-phase motor, the effective current being zero, and a second current to the single-phase motor, the second input current being the effective current that flows through the multi-phase motor. The device of claim 16, wherein the device is configured to drive at least the single-phase motor from the single-phase motor and the multi-phase motor, by turning on a plurality of the upper switching elements of the inverter and turning off remaining upper switching elements of the inverter, or turning on a plurality of the lower switching elements of the inverter and turning off remaining lower switching elements of the inverter. An electric apparatus comprising a device configured to drive a plurality of motors, the device comprising: an inverter connected to a direct current (DC) terminal; a multi-phase motor connected to the inverter; and a single-phase motor connected to the multi-phase motor, wherein the device is configured to drive the single-phase motor and the multi-phase motor at a same speed by inputting a first number of frequencies for current input to the multi-phase motor, wherein the device is configured to drive the single-phase motor and the multi-phase motor at different speeds by inputting a second number of frequencies for current input to the multi-phase motor, and wherein the first number is smaller than the second number.
